title: Berlin Train Lines
description: You are given three tables: train lines, stations, and stops linking trains to stations. Find distinct names of train lines that stop at a station located in 'Berlin', ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
trains(id, name, operator)
stations(id, name, city)
stops(id, train_id, station_id, stop_order)
outputFormat: One train name per line, ordered alphabetically. No duplicates.
constraints: Use two JOINs and DISTINCT. Filter WHERE city = 'Berlin'. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT DISTINCT t.name FROM trains t JOIN stops s ON t.id = s.train_id JOIN stations st ON s.station_id = st.id WHERE st.city = 'Berlin' ORDER BY t.name;
hint: Join trains → stops → stations, filter by city = 'Berlin', use DISTINCT.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE trains (id INTEGER, name TEXT, operator TEXT); CREATE TABLE stations (id INTEGER, name TEXT, city TEXT); CREATE TABLE stops (id INTEGER, train_id INTEGER, station_id INTEGER, stop_order INTEGER); INSERT INTO trains VALUES (1,'ICE 101','DB'),(2,'IC 202','DB'),(3,'RE 303','DB'); INSERT INTO stations VALUES (1,'Berlin Hbf','Berlin'),(2,'Hamburg Hbf','Hamburg'),(3,'Berlin Ostbf','Berlin'); INSERT INTO stops VALUES (1,1,1,1),(2,1,2,2),(3,2,2,1),(4,3,3,1),(5,3,1,2);

CREATE TABLE trains (id INTEGER, name TEXT, operator TEXT); CREATE TABLE stations (id INTEGER, name TEXT, city TEXT); CREATE TABLE stops (id INTEGER, train_id INTEGER, station_id INTEGER, stop_order INTEGER); INSERT INTO trains VALUES (1,'S1','BVG'),(2,'S2','BVG'),(3,'S3','BVG'),(4,'S4','BVG'); INSERT INTO stations VALUES (1,'Alexanderplatz','Berlin'),(2,'Munich Hbf','Munich'),(3,'Potsdam Hbf','Potsdam'); INSERT INTO stops VALUES (1,1,1,1),(2,2,2,1),(3,3,2,1),(4,4,1,1),(5,4,3,2);

CREATE TABLE trains (id INTEGER, name TEXT, operator TEXT); CREATE TABLE stations (id INTEGER, name TEXT, city TEXT); CREATE TABLE stops (id INTEGER, train_id INTEGER, station_id INTEGER, stop_order INTEGER); INSERT INTO trains VALUES (1,'EC 10','DB'),(2,'IC 20','DB'); INSERT INTO stations VALUES (1,'Frankfurt Hbf','Frankfurt'),(2,'Cologne Hbf','Cologne'); INSERT INTO stops VALUES (1,1,1,1),(2,2,2,1);

CREATE TABLE trains (id INTEGER, name TEXT, operator TEXT); CREATE TABLE stations (id INTEGER, name TEXT, city TEXT); CREATE TABLE stops (id INTEGER, train_id INTEGER, station_id INTEGER, stop_order INTEGER); INSERT INTO trains VALUES (1,'ICE 500','DB'); INSERT INTO stations VALUES (1,'Berlin Hbf','Berlin'),(2,'Hamburg Hbf','Hamburg'); INSERT INTO stops VALUES (1,1,1,1),(2,1,2,2);
